Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants a DuckDB SQL query to find the top K process variants and return all events from the cases that belong to these top K variants The event log table is named event log Columns case id activity timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 1 Construct the ordered sequence of activities for each case id We can use string agg activity ORDER BY timestamp or array agg activity ORDER BY timestamp

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Lets use string agg or array agg Actually string agg activity ORDER BY timestamp is standard

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 2 Group cases by their complete activity sequence to identify unique process variants

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Wait the prompt says top K variants I should use a placeholder or specific value for K like 5 or K

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 3 and 4 Return all events from the original event log that belong only to cases which are part of these top K variants

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait string agg might have issues if activity names contain commas Lets use a safer delimiter

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: This is cleaner and avoids the extra VariantCounts CTE

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets double check if string agg works with ORDER BY in DuckDB Yes string agg expression delimiter ORDER BY sort expression is supported in DuckDB

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Alternatively array agg activity ORDER BY timestamp is also fine

Name: Validation - PE
Text: What if timestamp has ties The prompt says determined by sorting events within that case by their timestamp so ORDER BY timestamp is sufficient
